name: Community Housing Council Of Fresno
description_short: Non-profit housing counseling organization providing free and low-cost assistance to California families for homeownership, rental support, and financial management.
description_long:
Community Housing Council of Fresno is a member-based non-profit organization dedicated to helping California families find and maintain stable housing. Founded to address housing insecurity and homeownership barriers in the Fresno region, CHC operates as a HUD-approved housing counseling agency with certified advisors on staff. The organization has served thousands of families facing housing-related financial challenges over many years of operation. Their work spans the full spectrum of housing needs, from renters facing eviction to first-time homebuyers and current homeowners navigating financial difficulties.

CHC offers comprehensive, free or low-cost services including housing counseling, first-time homebuyer education classes, financial management workshops, and disaster preparedness training. They provide one-on-one counseling with certified housing counselors available by phone at (559) 221-6919 for personalized guidance. The organization previously administered the California Mortgage Relief Program but is no longer accepting new applications for that initiative. Classes and workshops are offered both in-person at their Fresno classroom and online with live instructors, making services accessible to working families. They also maintain a Real Estate Learning Center and blog providing educational resources.

What distinguishes CHC is their focus on California families specifically and their integrated approach addressing renters, current homeowners, and future homeowners through a single organization. Their membership model invites community participation and investment in housing solutions. Unlike for-profit lenders, CHC prioritizes accessibility and education over revenue generation, offering many services at no cost. They maintain partnerships with other organizations to expand their reach and impact.

The main limitation is that CHC is not currently accepting new grant applications and has suspended intake for certain relief programs. Their services appear concentrated in the Fresno area, which may limit accessibility for consumers in other California regions. As a non-profit with finite resources, wait times for counselor availability may exist during peak demand periods. However, for eligible consumers in their service area seeking genuine financial guidance without sales pressure, CHC represents a trustworthy, non-predatory resource.
pros: Free or low-cost housing counseling with certified HUD-approved advisors
Comprehensive services for entire housing journey: renters, homeowners, and first-time buyers
Classes and workshops offered both in-person and online with live instructors
Member-based non-profit structure ensures focus on consumer benefit, not profit maximization
Direct phone access to housing counselors at (559) 221-6919 for personalized guidance
Educational resources including Real Estate Learning Center and blog for self-directed learning
No pressure sales environment—mission-driven approach to housing assistance
cons: Currently not accepting new grant applications, limiting financial relief options
California Mortgage Relief Program no longer accepting new applicants
Services appear geographically concentrated in Fresno area, potentially limiting regional access
No clear information about counselor availability or average wait times for appointments
Limited transparency on specific income thresholds or eligibility requirements on website
best_for: First-time homebuyers in California seeking education and pre-purchase counseling
Renters facing eviction seeking legal and financial guidance
Current homeowners struggling with mortgage payments or financial challenges
Families seeking free financial management education and disaster preparedness training
diagnosis: CHC is best for California residents seeking genuine, non-commercial housing education and counseling without predatory lending pressure. The primary caveat is that grant applications are currently closed and some relief programs are not accepting new applicants, so consumers should call to confirm which specific services are available at the time of their inquiry.
services: Free housing counseling with certified advisors
First-time homebuyer classes (Are You Ready program)
Live online and in-person educational classes
Educational blog with housing and finance content
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 2560 W Shaw Ln Ste 101, Fresno, CA 93711
meta_description: Community Housing Council of Fresno: non-profit providing free housing counseling for homeownership, rental support, and financial management.
